Scenario API reference¶

scenario.State¶

class scenario.state.Action(name: str, params: ~typing.Dict[str, AnyJson] = <factory>, id: str = <factory>)[source]¶

Bases: _DCBase

A juju run command.

Used to simulate juju run, passing in any parameters. For example:

def test_backup_action():
    action = scenario.Action('do_backup', params={'filename': 'foo'})
    ctx = scenario.Context(MyCharm)
    out: scenario.ActionOutput = ctx.run_action(action, scenario.State())
property event: Event¶

Helper to generate an action event from this action.

id: str¶

Juju action ID.

Every action invocation is automatically assigned a new one. Override in the rare cases where a specific ID is required.

name: str¶

Juju action name, as found in the charm metadata.

params: Dict[str, AnyJson]¶

Parameter values passed to the action.

class scenario.state.Address(hostname: str, value: str, cidr: str, address: str = '')[source]¶

Bases: _DCBase

address: str = ''¶
cidr: str¶
hostname: str¶
value: str¶
class scenario.state.BindAddress(interface_name: str, addresses: List[scenario.state.Address], mac_address: str | None = None)[source]¶

Bases: _DCBase

addresses: List[Address]¶
hook_tool_output_fmt()[source]¶
interface_name: str¶
mac_address: str | None = None¶
exception scenario.state.BindFailedError[source]¶

Bases: RuntimeError

Raised when Event.bind fails.

class scenario.state.Container(name: str, can_connect: bool = False, _base_plan: dict = <factory>, layers: ~typing.Dict[str, ~ops.pebble.Layer] = <factory>, service_status: ~typing.Dict[str, ~ops.pebble.ServiceStatus] = <factory>, mounts: ~typing.Dict[str, ~scenario.state.Mount] = <factory>, exec_mock: ~typing.Dict[~typing.Tuple[str, ...], ~scenario.state.ExecOutput] = <factory>)[source]¶

Bases: _DCBase

A Kubernetes container where a charm’s workload runs.

can_connect: bool = False¶

When False, all Pebble operations will fail.

exec_mock: Dict[Tuple[str, ...], ExecOutput]¶

Simulate executing commands in the container.

Specify each command the charm might run in the container and a ExecOutput containing its return code and any stdout/stderr.

For example:

container = scenario.Container(
    name='foo',
    exec_mock={
        ('whoami', ): scenario.ExecOutput(return_code=0, stdout='ubuntu')
        ('dig', '+short', 'canonical.com'):
            scenario.ExecOutput(return_code=0, stdout='185.125.190.20\n185.125.190.21')
    }
)
get_filesystem(ctx: Context) → Path[source]¶

Simulated Pebble filesystem in this context.

Returns:

A temporary filesystem containing any files or directories the charm pushed to the container.

layers: Dict[str, Layer]¶

All ops.pebble.Layer definitions that have already been added to the container.

mounts: Dict[str, Mount]¶

Provides access to the contents of the simulated container filesystem.

For example, suppose you want to express that your container has:

  • /home/foo/bar.py

  • /bin/bash

  • /bin/baz

this becomes:

mounts = {
    'foo': scenario.Mount('/home/foo',  Path('/path/to/local/dir/containing/bar/py/')),
    'bin': Mount('/bin/', Path('/path/to/local/dir/containing/bash/and/baz/')),
}
name: str¶

Name of the container, as found in the charm metadata.

property pebble_ready_event¶

Sugar to generate a <this container’s name>-pebble-ready event.

property plan: Plan¶

The ‘computed’ Pebble plan.

i.e. the base plan plus the layers that have been added on top. You should run your assertions on this plan, not so much on the layers, as those are input data.

service_status: Dict[str, ServiceStatus]¶

The current status of each Pebble service running in the container.

property services: Dict[str, ServiceInfo]¶

The Pebble services as rendered in the plan.

class scenario.state.DeferredEvent(handle_path: str, owner: str, observer: str, snapshot_data: Dict = <factory>)[source]¶

Bases: _DCBase

handle_path: str¶
property name¶
observer: str¶
owner: str¶
snapshot_data: Dict¶
class scenario.state.Event(path: str, args: Tuple[Any, ...] = (), kwargs: Dict[str, Any] = <factory>, storage: Optional[ForwardRef('Storage')] = None, relation: Optional[ForwardRef('AnyRelation')] = None, relation_remote_unit_id: Optional[int] = None, secret: Optional[scenario.state.Secret] = None, container: Optional[scenario.state.Container] = None, action: Optional[ForwardRef('Action')] = None, _owner_path: List[str] = <factory>)[source]¶

Bases: _DCBase

action: Action | None = None¶
args: Tuple[Any, ...] = ()¶
bind(state: State)[source]¶

Attach to this event the state component it needs.

For example, a relation event initialized without a Relation instance will search for a suitable relation in the provided state and return a copy of itself with that relation attached.

In case of ambiguity (e.g. multiple relations found on ‘foo’ for event ‘foo-relation-changed’, we pop a warning and bind the first one. Use with care!

container: Container | None = None¶
deferred(handler: Callable, event_id: int = 1) → DeferredEvent[source]¶

Construct a DeferredEvent from this Event.

kwargs: Dict[str, Any]¶
property name: str¶

Full event name.

Consists of a ‘prefix’ and a ‘suffix’. The suffix denotes the type of the event, the prefix the name of the entity the event is about.

“foo-relation-changed”:

  • “foo”=prefix (name of a relation),

  • “-relation-changed”=suffix (relation event)

property owner_path: List[str]¶

Path to the ObjectEvents instance owning this event.

If this event is defined on the toplevel charm class, it should be [‘on’].

path: str¶
relation: AnyRelation | None = None¶
relation_remote_unit_id: int | None = None¶
secret: Secret | None = None¶
storage: Storage | None = None¶
class scenario.state.ExecOutput(return_code: int = 0, stdout: str = '', stderr: str = '', _change_id: int = <factory>)[source]¶

Bases: object

Mock data for simulated ops.Container.exec() calls.

return_code: int = 0¶

The return code of the process (0 is success).

stderr: str = ''¶

Any content written to stderr by the process.

stdout: str = ''¶

Any content written to stdout by the process.

class scenario.state.JujuLogLine(level, message)¶

Bases: tuple

A simulated line in Juju’s debug-log.

level¶

The log level, roughly equivalent to logging.Logger levels.

message¶

The formatted log message.

level¶

Alias for field number 0

message¶

Alias for field number 1

exception scenario.state.MetadataNotFoundError[source]¶

Bases: RuntimeError

Raised when Scenario can’t find a metadata.yaml file in the provided charm root.

class scenario.state.Model(name: str = <factory>, uuid: str = <factory>, type: ~typing.Literal['kubernetes', 'lxd'] = 'kubernetes')[source]¶

Bases: _DCBase

The Juju model in which the charm is deployed.

name: str¶

The name of the model.

type: Literal['kubernetes', 'lxd'] = 'kubernetes'¶

The type of Juju model.

uuid: str¶

A unique identifier for the model, typically generated by Juju.

class scenario.state.Mount(location: str | PurePosixPath, src: str | Path)[source]¶

Bases: _DCBase

Maps local files to a Container filesystem.

location: str | PurePosixPath¶

The location inside of the container.

src: str | Path¶

The content to provide when the charm does ops.Container.pull().

class scenario.state.Network(bind_addresses: List[scenario.state.BindAddress], ingress_addresses: List[str], egress_subnets: List[str])[source]¶

Bases: _DCBase

bind_addresses: List[BindAddress]¶
classmethod default(private_address: str = '192.0.2.0', hostname: str = '', cidr: str = '', interface_name: str = '', mac_address: str | None = None, egress_subnets=('192.0.2.0/24',), ingress_addresses=('192.0.2.0',)) → Network[source]¶

Helper to create a minimal, heavily defaulted Network.

egress_subnets: List[str]¶
hook_tool_output_fmt()[source]¶
ingress_addresses: List[str]¶
class scenario.state.PeerRelation(endpoint: str, interface: str | None = None, relation_id: int = <factory>, local_app_data: RawDataBagContents = <factory>, local_unit_data: RawDataBagContents = <factory>, peers_data: ~typing.Dict[UnitID, RawDataBagContents] = <factory>)[source]¶

Bases: RelationBase

A relation to share data between units of the charm.

peers_data: Dict[UnitID, RawDataBagContents]¶

Current contents of the peer databags.

class scenario.state.Port(protocol: Literal['tcp', 'udp', 'icmp'], port: int | None = None)[source]¶

Bases: _DCBase

Represents a port on the charm host.

port: int | None = None¶

The port to open. Required for TCP and UDP; not allowed for ICMP.

protocol: Literal['tcp', 'udp', 'icmp']¶

The protocol that data transferred over the port will use.

class scenario.state.Relation(endpoint: str, interface: str | None = None, relation_id: int = <factory>, local_app_data: RawDataBagContents = <factory>, local_unit_data: RawDataBagContents = <factory>, remote_app_name: str = 'remote', limit: int = 1, remote_app_data: RawDataBagContents = <factory>, remote_units_data: ~typing.Dict[UnitID, RawDataBagContents] = <factory>)[source]¶

Bases: RelationBase

An integration between the charm and another application.

limit: int = 1¶

The maximum number of integrations on this endpoint.

remote_app_data: RawDataBagContents¶

The current content of the application databag.

remote_app_name: str = 'remote'¶

The name of the remote application, as in the charm’s metadata.

remote_units_data: Dict[UnitID, RawDataBagContents]¶

The current content of the databag for each unit in the relation.

class scenario.state.RelationBase(endpoint: str, interface: Optional[str] = None, relation_id: int = <factory>, local_app_data: 'RawDataBagContents' = <factory>, local_unit_data: 'RawDataBagContents' = <factory>)[source]¶

Bases: _DCBase

property broken_event: Event¶

Sugar to generate a <this relation>-relation-broken event.

property changed_event: Event¶

Sugar to generate a <this relation>-relation-changed event.

property created_event: Event¶

Sugar to generate a <this relation>-relation-created event.

property departed_event: Event¶

Sugar to generate a <this relation>-relation-departed event.

endpoint: str¶

Relation endpoint name. Must match some endpoint name defined in metadata.yaml.

interface: str | None = None¶

Interface name. Must match the interface name attached to this endpoint in metadata.yaml. If left empty, it will be automatically derived from metadata.yaml.

property joined_event: Event¶

Sugar to generate a <this relation>-relation-joined event.

local_app_data: RawDataBagContents¶

This application’s databag for this relation.

local_unit_data: RawDataBagContents¶

This unit’s databag for this relation.

relation_id: int¶

Juju relation ID. Every new Relation instance gets a unique one, if there’s trouble, override.

class scenario.state.Secret(id: str, contents: Dict[int, ForwardRef('RawSecretRevisionContents')], owner: Literal['unit', 'app', None] = None, granted: Any = '<DEPRECATED>', revision: int = 0, remote_grants: Dict[int, Set[str]] = <factory>, label: Optional[str] = None, description: Optional[str] = None, expire: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None, rotate: Optional[ops.model.SecretRotate] = None)[source]¶

Bases: _DCBase

property changed_event¶

Sugar to generate a secret-changed event.

contents: Dict[int, RawSecretRevisionContents]¶
description: str | None = None¶
expire: datetime | None = None¶
property expired_event¶

Sugar to generate a secret-expired event.

granted: Any = '<DEPRECATED>'¶
id: str¶
label: str | None = None¶
owner: Literal['unit', 'app', None] = None¶
remote_grants: Dict[int, Set[str]]¶
property remove_event¶

Sugar to generate a secret-remove event.

revision: int = 0¶
rotate: SecretRotate | None = None¶
property rotate_event¶

Sugar to generate a secret-rotate event.

class scenario.state.State(config: ~typing.Dict[str, str | int | float | bool] = <factory>, relations: ~typing.List[AnyRelation] = <factory>, networks: ~typing.Dict[str, ~scenario.state.Network] = <factory>, containers: ~typing.List[~scenario.state.Container] = <factory>, storage: ~typing.List[~scenario.state.Storage] = <factory>, opened_ports: ~typing.List[~scenario.state.Port] = <factory>, leader: bool = False, model: ~scenario.state.Model = Model(name='yhTVP8V7ZJn3ArCUdYHj', uuid='a8ea9d46-4de2-40ad-a669-802b7f262848', type='kubernetes'), secrets: ~typing.List[~scenario.state.Secret] = <factory>, resources: ~typing.Dict[str, PathLike] = <factory>, planned_units: int = 1, deferred: ~typing.List[DeferredEvent] = <factory>, stored_state: ~typing.List[StoredState] = <factory>, app_status: ~ops.model.StatusBase | ~scenario.state._EntityStatus = UnknownStatus(), unit_status: ~ops.model.StatusBase | ~scenario.state._EntityStatus = UnknownStatus(), workload_version: str = '')[source]¶

Bases: _DCBase

Represents the juju-owned portion of a unit’s state.

Roughly speaking, it wraps all hook-tool- and pebble-mediated data a charm can access in its lifecycle. For example, status-get will return data from State.status, is-leader will return data from State.leader, and so on.

app_status: StatusBase | _EntityStatus = UnknownStatus()¶

Status of the application.

config: Dict[str, str | int | float | bool]¶

The present configuration of this charm.

containers: List[Container]¶

All containers (whether they can connect or not) that this charm is aware of.

deferred: List[DeferredEvent]¶

Events that have been deferred on this charm by some previous execution.

get_container(container: str | Container) → Container[source]¶

Get container from this State, based on an input container or its name.

get_relations(endpoint: str) → Tuple[AnyRelation, ...][source]¶

Get all relations on this endpoint from the current state.

get_storages(name: str) → Tuple[Storage, ...][source]¶

Get all storages with this name.

jsonpatch_delta(other: State)[source]¶
leader: bool = False¶

Whether this charm has leadership.

model: Model = Model(name='yhTVP8V7ZJn3ArCUdYHj', uuid='a8ea9d46-4de2-40ad-a669-802b7f262848', type='kubernetes')¶

The model this charm lives in.

networks: Dict[str, Network]¶

Manual overrides for any relation and extra bindings currently provisioned for this charm. If a metadata-defined relation endpoint is not explicitly mapped to a Network in this field, it will be defaulted. [CAVEAT: extra-bindings is a deprecated, regretful feature in juju/ops. For completeness we support it, but use at your own risk.] If a metadata-defined extra-binding is left empty, it will be defaulted.

opened_ports: List[Port]¶

Ports opened by juju on this charm.

planned_units: int = 1¶

Number of non-dying planned units that are expected to be running this application. Use with caution.

relations: List[AnyRelation]¶

All relations that currently exist for this charm.

resources: Dict[str, PathLike]¶

Mapping from resource name to path at which the resource can be found.

secrets: List[Secret]¶

The secrets this charm has access to (as an owner, or as a grantee). The presence of a secret in this list entails that the charm can read it. Whether it can manage it or not depends on the individual secret’s owner flag.

storage: List[Storage]¶

All ATTACHED storage instances for this charm. If a storage is not attached, omit it from this listing.

stored_state: List[StoredState]¶

Contents of a charm’s stored state.

unit_status: StatusBase | _EntityStatus = UnknownStatus()¶

Status of the unit.

with_can_connect(container_name: str, can_connect: bool) → State[source]¶
with_leadership(leader: bool) → State[source]¶
with_unit_status(status: StatusBase) → State[source]¶
workload_version: str = ''¶

Workload version.

exception scenario.state.StateValidationError[source]¶

Bases: RuntimeError

Raised when individual parts of the State are inconsistent.

class scenario.state.Storage(name: str, index: int = <factory>)[source]¶

Bases: _DCBase

Represents an (attached!) storage made available to the charm container.

Sugar to generate a <this storage>-storage-attached event.

property detaching_event: Event¶

Sugar to generate a <this storage>-storage-detached event.

get_filesystem(ctx: Context) → Path[source]¶

Simulated filesystem root in this context.

index: int¶
name: str¶

data_type_name: str = 'StoredStateData'¶

class scenario.state.SubordinateRelation(endpoint: str, interface: Optional[str] = None, relation_id: int = <factory>, local_app_data: 'RawDataBagContents' = <factory>, local_unit_data: 'RawDataBagContents' = <factory>, remote_app_data: 'RawDataBagContents' = <factory>, remote_unit_data: 'RawDataBagContents' = <factory>, remote_app_name: str = 'remote', remote_unit_id: int = 0)[source]¶

remote_app_data: RawDataBagContents¶

scenario.state.deferred(event: str | Event, handler: Callable, event_id: int = 1, relation: Relation | None = None, container: Container | None = None)[source]¶

Construct a DeferredEvent from an Event or an event name.

scenario.state.next_action_id(update=True)[source]¶
scenario.state.next_relation_id(update=True)[source]¶
scenario.state.next_storage_index(update=True)[source]¶

Get the index (used to be called ID) the next Storage to be created will get.

Pass update=False if you’re only inspecting it. Pass update=True if you also want to bump it.

scenario.state.normalize_name(s: str)[source]¶

Event names, in Scenario, uniformly use underscores instead of dashes.

scenario.state.sort_patch(patch: ~typing.List[~typing.Dict], key=<function <lambda>>)[source]¶

scenario.Context¶

class scenario.context.ActionOutput(state: State, logs: List[str], results: Dict[str, Any] | None, failure: str | None = None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Wraps the results of running an action event with run_action.

failure: str | None = None¶

None if the action was successful, otherwise the message the charm set with ops.ActionEvent.fail().

logs: List[str]¶

Any logs associated with the action output, set by the charm with ops.ActionEvent.log().

results: Dict[str, Any] | None¶

Key-value mapping assigned by the charm as a result of the action. Will be None if the charm never calls ops.ActionEvent.set_results().

state: State¶

The charm state after the action has been handled.

In most cases, actions are not expected to be affecting it.

property success: bool¶

True if this action was a success, False otherwise.

exception scenario.context.AlreadyEmittedError[source]¶

Bases: RuntimeError

Raised when run() is called more than once.

class scenario.context.Context(charm_type: Type[CharmType], meta: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, actions: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, config: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, charm_root: PathLike | None = None, juju_version: str = '3.4', capture_deferred_events: bool = False, capture_framework_events: bool = False, app_name: str | None = None, unit_id: int | None = 0)[source]¶

Bases: object

Represents a simulated charm’s execution context.

It is the main entry point to running a scenario test.

It contains: the charm source code being executed, the metadata files associated with it, a charm project repository root, and the Juju version to be simulated.

After you have instantiated Context, typically you will call one of run() or run_action() to execute the charm once, write any assertions you like on the output state returned by the call, write any assertions you like on the Context attributes, then discard the Context.

Each Context instance is in principle designed to be single-use: Context is not cleaned up automatically between charm runs. You can call .clear() to do some clean up, but we don’t guarantee all state will be gone.

Any side effects generated by executing the charm, that are not rightful part of the State, are in fact stored in the Context:

  • juju_log: record of what the charm has sent to juju-log

  • app_status_history: record of the app statuses the charm has set

  • unit_status_history: record of the unit statuses the charm has set

  • workload_version_history: record of the workload versions the charm has set

  • emitted_events: record of the events (including custom) that the charm has processed

This allows you to write assertions not only on the output state, but also, to some extent, on the path the charm took to get there.

A typical scenario test will look like:

from scenario import Context, State
from ops import ActiveStatus
from charm import MyCharm, MyCustomEvent  # noqa

def test_foo():
    # Arrange: set the context up
    c = Context(MyCharm)
    # Act: prepare the state and emit an event
    state_out = c.run('update-status', State())
    # Assert: verify the output state is what you think it should be
    assert state_out.unit_status == ActiveStatus('foobar')
    # Assert: verify the Context contains what you think it should
    assert len(c.emitted_events) == 4
    assert isinstance(c.emitted_events[3], MyCustomEvent)

If the charm, say, expects a ./src/foo/bar.yaml file present relative to the execution cwd, you need to use the charm_root argument. For example:

import scenario
import tempfile
virtual_root = tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()
local_path = Path(local_path.name)
(local_path / 'foo').mkdir()
(local_path / 'foo' / 'bar.yaml').write_text('foo: bar')
scenario.Context(... charm_root=virtual_root).run(...)
Parameters:

  • charm_type – the CharmBase subclass to call ops.main() on.

  • meta – charm metadata to use. Needs to be a valid metadata.yaml format (as a dict). If none is provided, we will search for a metadata.yaml file in the charm root.

  • actions – charm actions to use. Needs to be a valid actions.yaml format (as a dict). If none is provided, we will search for a actions.yaml file in the charm root.

  • config – charm config to use. Needs to be a valid config.yaml format (as a dict). If none is provided, we will search for a config.yaml file in the charm root.

  • juju_version – Juju agent version to simulate.

  • app_name – App name that this charm is deployed as. Defaults to the charm name as defined in its metadata

  • unit_id – Unit ID that this charm is deployed as. Defaults to 0.

  • charm_root – virtual charm root the charm will be executed with.

action_manager(action: Action | str, state: State)[source]¶

Context manager to introspect live charm object before and after the event is emitted.

Usage: >>> with Context().action_manager(“foo-action”, State()) as manager: >>> assert manager.charm._some_private_attribute == “foo” # noqa >>> manager.run() # this will fire the event >>> assert manager.charm._some_private_attribute == “bar” # noqa

Parameters:

  • action – the Action that the charm will execute. Can be a string or an Action instance.

  • state – the State instance to use as data source for the hook tool calls that the charm will invoke when handling the Action (event).

cleanup()[source]¶

Cleanup side effects histories and reset the simulated filesystem state.

clear()[source]¶

Deprecated.

Use cleanup instead.

manager(event: Event | str, state: State)[source]¶

Context manager to introspect live charm object before and after the event is emitted.

Usage:

with Context().manager("start", State()) as manager:
    assert manager.charm._some_private_attribute == "foo"  # noqa
    manager.run()  # this will fire the event
    assert manager.charm._some_private_attribute == "bar"  # noqa
Parameters:

  • event – the Event that the charm will respond to.

  • state – the State instance to use when handling the Event.

property output_state: State¶

The output state obtained by running an event on this context.

Raises:

RuntimeError – if this Context hasn’t been run() yet.

run(event: Event | str, state: State, pre_event: Callable[[CharmBase], None] | None = None, post_event: Callable[[CharmBase], None] | None = None) → State[source]¶

Trigger a charm execution with an Event and a State.

Calling this function will call ops.main and set up the context according to the specified State, then emit the event on the charm.

Parameters:

  • event – the Event that the charm will respond to. Can be a string or an Event instance.

  • state – the State instance to use as data source for the hook tool calls that the charm will invoke when handling the Event.

  • pre_event – callback to be invoked right before emitting the event on the newly instantiated charm. Will receive the charm instance as only positional argument. This argument is deprecated. Please use Context.manager instead.

  • post_event – callback to be invoked right after emitting the event on the charm. Will receive the charm instance as only positional argument. This argument is deprecated. Please use Context.manager instead.

run_action(action: Action | str, state: State, pre_event: Callable[[CharmBase], None] | None = None, post_event: Callable[[CharmBase], None] | None = None) → ActionOutput[source]¶

Trigger a charm execution with an Action and a State.

Calling this function will call ops.main and set up the context according to the specified State, then emit the event on the charm.

Parameters:

  • action – the Action that the charm will execute. Can be a string or an Action instance.

  • state – the State instance to use as data source for the hook tool calls that the charm will invoke when handling the Action (event).

  • pre_event – callback to be invoked right before emitting the event on the newly instantiated charm. Will receive the charm instance as only positional argument. This argument is deprecated. Please use Context.action_manager instead.

  • post_event – callback to be invoked right after emitting the event on the charm. Will receive the charm instance as only positional argument. This argument is deprecated. Please use Context.action_manager instead.

exception scenario.context.ContextSetupError[source]¶

Bases: RuntimeError

Raised by Context when setup fails.

exception scenario.context.InvalidActionError[source]¶

Bases: InvalidEventError

raised when something is wrong with the action passed to Context.run_action

exception scenario.context.InvalidEventError[source]¶

Bases: RuntimeError

raised when something is wrong with the event passed to Context.run_*

scenario.consistency_checker¶

class scenario.consistency_checker.Results(errors: Iterable[str], warnings: Iterable[str])[source]¶

Bases: NamedTuple

Consistency checkers return type.

errors: Iterable[str]¶

Alias for field number 0

warnings: Iterable[str]¶

Alias for field number 1

scenario.consistency_checker.check_config_consistency(*, state: State, charm_spec: _CharmSpec, juju_version: Tuple[int, ...], **_kwargs) → Results[source]¶

Check the consistency of the state.config with the charm_spec.config (config.yaml).

scenario.consistency_checker.check_consistency(state: State, event: Event, charm_spec: _CharmSpec, juju_version: str)[source]¶

Validate the combination of a state, an event, a charm spec, and a juju version.

When invoked, it performs a series of checks that validate that the state is consistent with itself, with the event being emitted, the charm metadata, etc…

This function performs some basic validation of the combination of inputs that goes into a scenario test and determines if the scenario is a realistic/plausible/consistent one.

A scenario is inconsistent if it can practically never occur because it contradicts the juju model. For example: juju guarantees that upon calling config-get, a charm will only ever get the keys it declared in its config.yaml. So a State declaring some config keys that are not in the charm’s config.yaml is nonsense, and the combination of the two is inconsistent.

scenario.consistency_checker.check_containers_consistency(*, state: State, event: Event, charm_spec: _CharmSpec, **_kwargs) → Results[source]¶

Check the consistency of state.containers vs. charm_spec.meta.

scenario.consistency_checker.check_event_consistency(*, event: Event, charm_spec: _CharmSpec, **_kwargs) → Results[source]¶

Check the internal consistency of the Event data structure.

For example, it checks that a relation event has a relation instance, and that the relation endpoint name matches the event prefix.

scenario.consistency_checker.check_network_consistency(*, state: State, event: Event, charm_spec: _CharmSpec, **_kwargs) → Results[source]¶
scenario.consistency_checker.check_relation_consistency(*, state: State, event: Event, charm_spec: _CharmSpec, **_kwargs) → Results[source]¶
scenario.consistency_checker.check_resource_consistency(*, state: State, charm_spec: _CharmSpec, **_kwargs) → Results[source]¶

Check the internal consistency of the resources from metadata and in State.

scenario.consistency_checker.check_secrets_consistency(*, event: Event, state: State, juju_version: Tuple[int, ...], **_kwargs) → Results[source]¶

Check the consistency of Secret-related stuff.

scenario.consistency_checker.check_storages_consistency(*, state: State, charm_spec: _CharmSpec, **_kwargs) → Results[source]¶

Check the consistency of the state.storages with the charm_spec.metadata (metadata.yaml).

scenario.capture_events¶

scenario.capture_events.capture_events(*types: Type[EventBase], include_framework=False, include_deferred=True)[source]¶

Capture all events of type *types (using instance checks).

Arguments exposed so that you can define your own fixtures if you want to.

Example:: >>> from ops.charm import StartEvent >>> from scenario import Event, State >>> from charm import MyCustomEvent, MyCharm # noqa >>> >>> def test_my_event(): >>> with capture_events(StartEvent, MyCustomEvent) as captured: >>> trigger(State(), (“start”, MyCharm, meta=MyCharm.META) >>> >>> assert len(captured) == 2 >>> e1, e2 = captured >>> assert isinstance(e2, MyCustomEvent) >>> assert e2.custom_attr == ‘foo’
